questions.subject: linear-programming
questions.prompt_md: Formulate the bipartite matching problem as a linear program and prove that the LP relaxation has an integral optimal solution.

sources.quote: LP formulation of bipartite matching appeared

questions.rationale_md: Core LP topic. Appeared on recent 2025 exam, featured prominently in maman 15 and lecture 3. Formulation + integrality argument is a classic 29-point question.

trend.reason: Recent exam emphasis signals continued relevance

questions.subject: data-structures
questions.prompt_md: Describe the Splay tree splay operation and prove the amortized O(log n) access bound using the potential method.
questions.rationale_md: Amortized analysis of splay trees is a recurring theme. Covered in lecture 2 and maman 12, with past exam appearances.

questions.subject: expander-graphs
questions.prompt_md: State and prove the relationship between the second eigenvalue of the adjacency matrix and the edge expansion of a d-regular graph (Cheeger inequality).
questions.rationale_md: Spectral characterization is central to the expander graphs unit. Recent 2025 exam confirms continued emphasis.

questions.subject: data-structures
questions.prompt_md: Analyze the amortized cost of DECREASE-KEY and EXTRACT-MIN in a Fibonacci heap.
questions.rationale_md: Fibonacci heaps are tested regularly. Smaller 13-point slot likely.

questions.subject: linear-programming
questions.prompt_md: Given a primal LP, write its dual and state the weak duality theorem. Use complementary slackness to verify optimality of a given pair.
questions.rationale_md: Duality mechanics show up in almost every exam variation, typically as a shorter 13-point question.
